pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 76-90 (build tags the flawed image as legacy-app:unsafe after wiring secret)
export_result.sh lines 17-82 (verifier checks for legacy-app:safe and uses it for secret and metadata validation)
setup_task.sh lines 49-74 & export_result.sh lines 21-31 (secrets.txt creation and docker save grep for the string)
setup_task.sh lines 69-73 & export_result.sh lines 40-54 (Dockerfile enforces APP_COLOR=blue and verifier checks for Env entry)
setup_task.sh lines 69-73 & export_result.sh lines 40-54 (Dockerfile exposes/ENV PORT 5000 and verifier asserts 5000/tcp exposed port)

privileged_info_summary: setup_task.sh generates the app, writes AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA_TEST_SECRET_DO_NOT_USE to secrets.txt, then builds the unsafe image as legacy-app:unsafe with ENV PORT=5000, ENV APP_COLOR=blue, EXPOSE 5000, and CMD ["python", "app.py"]. export_result.sh then expects and inspects legacy-app:safe to confirm it carries that metadata, runs on port 5000, and contains no AKIA_TEST_SECRET_DO_NOT_USE when docker save is grepped.
